marc:datafield.marc:subfield: a portrait = Marie-Pierre Langlamet im Porträt /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.

"'There are no dividing lines between sound and silence on the harp,' says Marie-Pierre Langlamet, harpist with the Berliner Philharmoniker since 1993. In our film portrait, the French-born harpist tells us how difficult it was to find her ideal instrument, and how she has experienced working with the orchestra's various chief conductors"--Film page.
a portrait = Knut Weber im Porträt /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Torben Jacobsen, video director ; Volker Striemer, Thomas Kutschker, directors of photography ; Erik Koschnik, Christoph Franke, Alexander Feucht, audio ; Arndt Baumüller, editor.

'"I'm often happy when I play the cello,' says Knut Weber, who has been a member of the Berliner Philharmoniker since 1998. In our portrait, he introduces his unique instrument, which was built in Rome. He also tells us why an encounter with 'Yesterday' by the Beatles was a kind of epiphany for him, what particularly appeals to him besides playing in the orchestra, and where he finds relaxation from everyday concert life"--Film page.
a portrait = Madeleine Carruzzo im Porträt /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Sibylle Strobel, director ; Thomas Kutschker, director of photography ; Oliver Lumpe, audio ; Phillip Offa, editor.

"From Herbert von Karajan to Kirill Petrenko: Violinist Madeleine Carruzzo has been a member of the Berliner Philharmoniker for four decades. In our film portrait, the Swiss-born Carruzzo tells how she came to take lessons with one of the most renowned violin teachers via the guitar, and how she felt when she became the first woman to be offered a position with the Philharmoniker in 1982"--Film page.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and Frank Peter Zimmermann =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und Frank Peter Zimmermann /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Frank Peter Zimmermann, Kirill Petrenko, interviewees.

Benedikt von Bernstorff, director ; Jakob von Bernstorff, editor ; David-Simon Gross, director of photography ; Mischa Kissin, Wenzel Krah, sound ; Timo Hagemeister, Jakob von Bernstorff, production supervisors.

"It is more than a long-standing partnership that connects the Berliner Philharmoniker with violinist Frank Peter Zimmermann - it is a friendship. This interview film reflects the special closeness between soloist and orchestra as well as with chief conductor Kirill Petrenko, Alan Gilbert and Daniel Harding. It was made during the joint recording of the violin concertos by Beethoven, Berg and Bartók, about which Zimmermann says: 'A greater honour than recording these four concertos is hardly imaginable'--Film page.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Kirill Petrenko, Raphael Haeger, Sara Ezzell, Filip Markiewicz, Cornelia Gartemann, interviewees.

Witja Frank, director ; Anja Simon and Robert Staffl, directors of photography ; Hanni Welter, editor ; Joscha Baltes, sound engineer, Alexander Feucht, mix engineer ; Cindy Golla, event production management.

"Our Close-up series for the whole family focuses on Igor Stravinsky's fairytale ballet The Firebird. The film is full of energy, combining music, images and movement. Enjoy exclusive insights into chief conductor Kirill Petrenko's rehearsals with the Berliner Philharmoniker - and dancer Sara Ezzell introduces you to the world of ballet, dancing to Stravinsky's multi-coloured, sparkling music"--Film page.
conductors in conversation = "Hüter der Einheit" : Dirigenten im Gespräch /
a film by Benedikt von Bernstorff and Jakob von Bernstorff.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Simon Halsey, interviewer ; Iván Fischer, Paavo Järvi, Louis Langrée, Zubin Mehta, Andris Nelsons, Sir Simon Rattle, John Carewe, interviewees.
Benedikt von Bernstorff and Jakob von Bernstorff, directors ; Martin Baer, Boris Fromageot, Thomas Kutschker, George Nducha, Volker Striemer, directors of photography.

"The art of conducting has always been shrouded in mystery. In this film, Simon Halsey, who has worked closely with the Berliner Philharmoniker for many years as conductor of the Berlin Rundfunkchor, interviews renowned conductors about their views of this unique profession, the secret of good baton technique and the relationship between analysis and emotion when working with the masterpieces of music"--Film page.
a film by Magdalena Zięba-Schwind and Daniel Finkernagel.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Berliner Philharmoniker ; John Adams, Gustavo Dudamel, Alan Gilbert, Leila Josefowicz, Sir Simon Rattle, Peter Sellars, interviewees.
Magdalena Zięba-Schwind, director ; Daniel Finkernagel, writer ; Uli Peschke, editor.

"Among contemporary composers, John Adams is one of the great genre-crossing artists: his music impresses with incredible complexity, but at the same time conveys an unbounded energy that leaves no-one unaffected. In the 2016/17 season, Adams was Artist in Residence with the Berliner Philharmoniker. Our documentary Short Rides with John Adams follows this exciting transatlantic collaboration"--Film page.
A life in music - Felix Mendelssohn Bartholdy, Part 3 :
at the height of his fame = Ein Leben in Musik - Felix Mendelssohn Bartholdy, Teil 3: Auf dem Gipfel des Ruhms /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.

Berliner Philharmoniker ; Kirill Petrenko, Christian Thielemann, Sir Simon Rattle, conductors ; Vilde Frang, violin, Rundfunkchor Berlin ; Simon Rössler, presenter ; Dennis Grosse-Plankermann, voice-over.
Susanne Ziese, idea and script editor ; Christopher Rowe, camera and sound ; Magdalena Zięba-Schwind, production supervisor ; Friedrich Kille, editor ;

"When Felix Mendelssohn was appointed Kapellmeister of the Leipzig Gewandhaus orchestra in 1835, he was the most famous composer in Europe. In this, the third and final part of Mendelssohn's A Life in Music, Philharmoniker percussionist Simon Rössler explains how the composer professionalised the music business, why his famous violin concerto remained a sketch for years, and how it came about that he 'just vegetated like a shrub'"--Film page.
Wir setzen uns mit Tränen nieder.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Berliner Philharmoniker ; Kirill Petrenko, conductor and interviewee ; Ulrich Matthes, interviewee.
Thomas Kutschker, Ralph Netzer, camera ; Hanni Welter, editor.

"Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky's fantasy overture Romeo and Juliet is based on one of the most passionate love stories in world literature. In our Close-up, members of the Berliner Philharmoniker, young people and actor Ulrich Matthes share their thoughts on the themes of love, conflict and reconciliation and read from Shakespeare's texts. In between, the film offers exciting insights into the orchestral rehearsals led by chief conductor Kirill Petrenko"--Film page.
a portrait = Philipp Bohnen im Porträt /
a film by Benedikt von Bernstorff and Jakob von Bernstorff.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Benedikt von Bernstorff, director ; Jakob von Bernstorff, director, editor, sound ; Christopher Rowe, Thomas Kutschker, director of photography.

"Violinist Philipp Bohnen was initially a scholarship holder at the Karajan Academy and became a member of the Berliner Philharmoniker in 2008. In our film portrait from the series The Berliner Philharmoniker and their Instruments, the versatile musician tells us who influenced him during his training and why, in addition to playing in an orchestra, he is particularly passionate about chamber music"--Film page.
seeing the world through my eyes = Patricia Kopatchinskaja : Die Welt mit eigenen Augen sehen /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Katharina Bernstone, director ; Uli Peschke, editor.

"Our portrait film presents Patricia Kopatchinskaja, who the Berliner Philharmoniker appointed their 2021/22 Artist in Residence. In the interview, the violinist tells us what fascinates her about slipping into different roles and why the music world needs more new music. She can be heard performing excerpts of Schoenberg's Violin Concerto with Kirill Petrenko. In addition, the film offers insights into her work with the Karajan Academy and the preparations for the Late Night concert 'Nonsense in Residence'"--Film page.
A life in music - Felix Mendelssohn Bartholdy, Part 2 :
from Berliner to cosmopolite = Ein Leben in Musik - Felix Mendelssohn Bartholdy, Teil 2: Vom Berliner zum Weltbürger.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.

Berliner Philharmoniker ; Kirill Petrenko, Riccardo Chailly, Pablo Heras-Casado, Sir Simon Rattle, conductors ; Rundfunkchor Berlin ; Cornelia Gartemann, presenter ; Dennis Grosse-Plankermann, voice-over.
Christopher Rowe, camera and sound ; Friedrich Kille, editor.

"Following on from Felix Mendelssohn's childhood and youth in Berlin, Philharmoniker violinist Cornelia Gartemann sheds light on the composer's eventful travelling years in the second part of our series A Life in Music. On his way from Berliner to cosmopolite, Mendelssohn found creative inspiration in Scotland, a second home in London and not quite what he had hoped for in Italy..."--Film page.
A life in music - Felix Mendelssohn Bartholdy, Part 1 :
a child prodigy in Berlin = Ein Leben in Musik - Felix Mendelssohn Bartholdy, Teil 1 : Ein Wunderkind in Berlin.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.

Berliner Philharmoniker ; Kirill Petrenko, Claudio Abbado ; Sir Simon Rattle, conductors ; Magdalena Kožená, mezzo-soprano ; Daniel Stabrawa, violin ; Rundfunkchor Berlin ; Philipp Bohnen, presenter.

"Composers and their times are the focus of our film series A Life in Music. The first of three parts is devoted to the life and work of Felix Mendelssohn during his time in Berlin. Accompanied by stories from his childhood and youth, we hear here not only works by Mendelssohn himself, but also those that were of particular importance to him. Narrator Philipp Bohnen, violinist with the Philharmoniker, takes us back to Berlin in the 1820s"--Film page.
Erbarme dich, erbarme dich, mein Gott.
35 years concertmaster with the Berliner Philharmoniker = Daniel Stabrawa : 35 Jahre Konzertmeister bei den Berliner Philharmonikern /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Magdalena Zięba-Schwind, director ; Thomas Kutscher, Stephan Zwickirsch, camera ; Sascha Reymann, sound ; Kevin Steiner, editor ; Katharina Bernstone, production.

"At the end of the 2020/21 season, the Berliner Philharmoniker will bid farewell to Daniel Stabrawa. The Polish-born violinist became a member of the orchestra in 1983 and took over the important position of 1st concertmaster three years later. In our film portrait, Stabrawa talks about the tasks of the post, his memories of four chief conductors and his passion for chamber music"--Film page.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Berliner Philharmoniker ; Kirill Petrenko, conductor and interviewee.

"In the Close-up series, the Berliner Philharmoniker and chief conductor Kirill Petrenko take a very personal look at individual works - in rehearsals, in conversation and in concert. The first Close-up promises a spirit of optimism: for the 100th anniversary of Kurt Weill's Symphony in One Movement, we go on a voyage of discovery to Berlin in the early 1920s"--Film page.
the viola is my voice = Tabea Zimmermann: Die Viola ist meine Stimme /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Magdalena Zięba-Schwind, film director ; Imke Koseck, editor ; Christopher Rowe, Thomas Kutschker, camera.

"Almost no other viola player can make the instrument sing so soulfully or bring out its virtuoso potential as brilliantly as Tabea Zimmermann. The Berliner Philharmoniker appointed her as Artist in Residence for the 2020/21 season. In Magdalena Zięba-Schwind's film The viola is my voice, you can find out how Zimmermann copes with the fact that this season in particular, it was hardly possible to perform in front of an audience, what making music means to her, and why she is fascinated by Paul Hindemith"--Film page.
a portrait = Solène Kermarrec im Porträt / a film by Torben Jacobsen.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Torben Jacobsen, director ; Thomas Kutscher, camera ; Stephan Zwickirsch, sound ; Uli Peschke, editor.

"Solène Kermarrec actually wanted to learn the double bass. But at the music school in her home town of Brest, the five-year-old was advised to start with the smaller cello - changing was then never an option for her. After studying in Paris, Budapest and Berlin, the cellist found her first position in an orchestra with the Berliner Philharmoniker at the age of 23 - and at the same time a home, as she says. In this film portrait, Solène Kermarrec also reveals why she prefers to play in a group rather than alone, how different cellists's ideas of sound can be, and what her second great passion is besides music"--Film page.
stories from the golden twenties = Berlin im Licht : Geschichten aus den Goldenen Zwanzigern /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Andreas Morell, video director ; Henning Brümmer, director of photography ; Kurt Weill, composer ; Kevin Steiner, editor.

"Accompanied by historical film footage and the music of Kurt Weill, actress Dagmar Manzel tells the story of Berlin in the 1920s: a city that was characterised in equal measure by cultural splendour and economic misery, political instability and joie de vivre, light music and cutting-edge art. Berlin im Licht is the title of a festival that in 1928 turned night into day in the capital. Weill and Bertolt Brecht wrote the catchy tune of the same name"--Film page.
Turning point = Philharmoniker, Unsere Geschichte, Teil 3 : Zeitenwende /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Rudolf Watzel, Alexander Wedow, Andreas Blau, Thomas Brandis, Léon Spierer, Dietrich Gerhardt, Karl Leister, Cötz Teutsch, Wolfram Christ, Erich Hartmann, interviewees.
Eric Schulz, director ; Thomas Wellmann, editor.

"The third and final part of Eric Schulz's film series Philharmoniker - Unsere Geschichte deals with the late years of the Karajan era when, despite great success, conflicts between chief conductor and orchestra began to emerge. The separation from Karajan and the fall of the Berlin Wall occurred almost simultaneously. Historical recordings show once again how moving and unforgettable the first concert shortly afterwards was for everyone involved"--Film page.
the West Berlin years = Philharmoniker, Unsere Geschichte, Teil 2 : Die Westberliner Jahre /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Rudolf Watzel, Alexander Wedow, Andreas Blau, Thomas Brandis, Léon Spierer, Dietrich Gerhardt, Karl Leister, Cötz Teutsch, Wolfram Christ, Erich Hartmann, interviewees.
Eric Schulz, film director ; Thomas Wellmann, editor.

"At the beginning of the second episode of the series Philharmoniker - Unsere Geschichte, the orchestra moves into the newly built Philharmonie. Here, chief conductor Herbert von Karajan and his musicians develop the ethereally beautiful sound that has become the international trademark of the Berliner Philharmoniker. In our documentary, members of the orchestra recall these years, artistic highlights and Karajan's intriguing personality. The episode also includes rare archive recordings and rehearsal footage"--Film page.
the early years = Philharmoniker, Unsere Geschichte, Teil 1: Die frühen Jahre.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Rudolf Watzel, Alexander Wedow, Andreas Blau, Thomas Brandis, Léon Spierer, Dietrich Gerhardt, Karl Leister, Cötz Teutsch, Wolfram Christ, Erich Hartmann, interviewees.
Eric Schulz, film director ; Thomas Wellmann, editor.

"Contemporary witnesses of the Berliner Philharmoniker look back: on the history of their orchestra, on former chief conductors, on the development of musical performance. The first episode of this series, produced exclusively for the Digital Concert Hall, deals with the early years, the first recordings under the direction of Arthur Nikisch and, above all, Wilhelm Furtwängler. How did this conductor create his unique dramatic arcs? And what changed with the beginning of the Karajan era?"--Film page.
a portrait = Rainer Seegers im Porträt /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Torben Jacobsen, director ; Uli Peschke, editor.

"In the summer of 2019, Rainer Seegers said goodbye to the Berliner Philharmoniker, where the timpanist had been a member for 33 years. In our film portrait, the musician explains the technical and tonal characteristics of his instrument and talks about his relationship with the orchestra. He also reveals his second great passion besides music: collecting butterflies"--Film page.
The 12 cellists of the Berliner Philharmoniker =
a film by Enrique Sánchez Lansch.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
The 12 Cellists of the Berliner Philharmoniker.
Schleswig-Holstein Musik Festival, Christkirche Rendsburg, Deutschland
Lucerne Festival, KKL Luzern, Konzerthalle, Schweiz
National Centre of Performing Arts, Beijing, China
Shanghai Grand Theatre Performing Arts Centre, Shanghai, China
Enrique Sánchez Lansch, director ; Julia Oehring, editor.

"Their sound is unmistakable: since its foundation in 1972, the 12 Cellists of the Berliner Philharmoniker have achieved international fame. Stylistically, the ensemble is equally at home in jazz and tango as in contemporary works and arrangements of classical pieces. Enrique Sánchez Lansch's 2012 documentary Die 12 accompanies the ensemble on a concert tour to China and shows in rehearsal and concert recordings the process leading to the premiere of a commissioned composition by Sofia Gubaidulina"--Film page.
"It comes my way..." = Bernard Haitink: "It comes my way..." /
a film by Hans Haffmans and Joost Honselaar.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Hans Haffmans, narrator ; Bernard Haitink, conductor and interviewee.

"Bernard Haitink has been one of the most successful and popular figures in the world of classical music since the 1960s. Before he announced his retirement from the concert circuit in 2019, he was one of the Berliner Philharmoniker's main guest conductors for over 50 years. Through recordings of concerts, rehearsals and personal conversations, our film portrait provides an insight into the intellectual world of Haitink, who describes music as his 'elixir of life'"--Film page.
a portrait = Rachel Helleur-Simcock im Porträt /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
In English and German with English subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.

"Since 2009, cellist Rachel Helleur-Simcock has been a permanent member of the Berliner Philharmoniker, and was previously a member of their Karajan Academy. This portrait of the series The Berliner Philharmoniker and their Instruments shows the musician in symphony concerts and at work with her string quartet. Helleur-Simcock also describes her 18th-century Italian instrument whose sound she associates with wood and honey"--Film page.
a portrait = Amihai Grosz im Porträt /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.

"Jerusalem-born Amihai Grosz has been 1st principal viola with the Berliner Philharmoniker since 2010. In this film portrait from the Berliner Philharmoniker and their Instruments series, we see him as the section leader in his orchestra, as a soloist in Philharmoniker concerts, as a chamber musician, and in activities with his family. The young artist also talks about the remarkably warm sound of his instrument, which was made in the 16th century"--Film page.
Kirill Petrenko and the Berliner Philharmoniker :
dawn of a new era = Kirill Petrenko und die Berliner Philharmoniker : Am Beginn einer neuen Ära /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
In English and in German with English subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Berliner Philharmoniker ; Kirill Petrenko, conductor and interviewee ; Matthew Hunter, Sarah Willis, Albrecht Mayer, Emmanuel Pahud, Rainer Seegers, Olaf Maninger, Helena Madoka Berg, interviewees.
Eric Schulz, director ; Peter Klum, editor.

"Their joint concert appearances between the election of Kirill Petrenko as chief conductor of the Berliner Philharmoniker and his debut in his new position in August 2019 were experienced by all involved as unusually intensive and exhilarating. And they considerably increased the anticipation of the new Philharmoniker era. In this film, which was made shortly before Petrenko took office, members of the orchestra talk about their new chief conductor. They express the way in which he combines meticulous rehearsal work with unleashed passion in concert, and musical uncompromisingness with personal modesty. Concert excerpts show the orchestra and the conductor performing works by Tchaikovsky, Scriabin and Beethoven"--Film page.
echoing an era = Simon Rattle : Echos einer Ära /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
In Englsih and in German with English subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Berliner Philharmoniker ; Sir Simon Rattle, conductor and interviewee.
Eric Schulz, director ; Peter Klum, editor.

"Sir Simon Rattle was chief conductor of the Berliner Philharmoniker for 16 years, and the unusual length of this time alone gives his tenure the feeling of an "era". But also in a qualitative sense, this was a special chapter in the history of the orchestra. This is shown in Eric Schulz's documentary, which traces the many highlights and achievements of Sir Simon's Berlin years in interviews, concert excerpts and archive material"--Film page.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
In English and in German with English subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Berliner Philharmoniker ; Herbert von Karajan, conductor.

"The 1978 documentary Impressions of Herbert von Karajan was the first major Karajan film biography. Without glorifying him, the film shows the conductor's diverse interests and talents: not only as a musician but also - for example - as a lover of fast cars and aeroplanes. And as the film was made during Karajan's lifetime, it gives a very authentic impression of the period"--Film page.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Torben Jacobsen, director ; Robert Hentschel, editor.

"Markus Weidmann is the first bassoonist to be the subject of a film portrait in the series The Berliner Philharmoniker and their Instruments. Weidmann, who became a member of the orchestra in 1997, was formerly also a student at the Karajan Academy. In this film, the musician talks about the character of the deepest instrument in the woodwind family, as well as about the complicated process of making the mouthpieces by hand. Other topics include personal favourites in the repertoire, such as Béla Bartók's Concerto for Orchestra, and Weidmann's passion for photography"--Film page.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
In English and In German with English subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Berliner Philharmoniker ; Sir Simon Rattle, conductor and interviewee.
Hong Kong, Guangzhou, Wuhan, Shanghai, Seoul, Kawasaki, and Tokyo.
Magdalena Zięba-Schwind, director ; Adam Janisch, Christoph Franke, Jakob von Bernstorff, editors.

"In the form of a video diary, this short film documents the Berliner Philharmoniker's last Asia tour together with its chief conductor Simon Rattle in November 2017 - which makes it a special moment in the history of the orchestra, both musically and emotionally. We see evocative images of cities such as Hong Kong, Wuhan, Seoul and Tokyo, plus there are interviews with the musicians and many impressions from rehearsals, workshops and concerts"--Film page.
a portrait = Laurentius Dinca im Porträt /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.

"In the latest episode of our series The Berliner Philharmoniker and their Instruments, the Romanian-born violinist Laurentius Dinca, describes his violin as a medium to "show one's soul". Dinca, who also appears as a keen chamber musician in the film portrait, became a member of the Berliner Philharmoniker in 1984 under Herbert von Karajan, and Kirill Petrenko will be the fourth chief conductor he has performed with"--Film page.
a portrait = Christoph Hartmann im Porträt /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Torben Jacobsen, director ; Uli Peschke, editor.

"Oboist Christoph Hartmann has been a member of the Berliner Philharmoniker since 1992. Born in Landsberg in Bavaria, he is the focus of the latest episode of our series The Berliner Philharmoniker and their Instruments. In the film portrait, we learn that Hartmann obtains his instruments from Paris, about the production of the mouthpieces by hand, and about the special expressive possibilities of the oboe: 'We can play sad melodies incredibly beautifully.' The film also includes excerpts from works by Brahms, Bartók and Bach"--Film page.
a portrait = Raphael Haeger im Porträt /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Torben Jacobsen, director ; Robert Hentschel, editor.

"Percussionists have a special status in the orchestra - if only because many of them have their musical roots in jazz and rock music. But most of all because they have to master not just one instrument, but a whole arsenal - from vibraphone to castanets. All this is told in this portrait from the series The Berliner Philharmoniker and their Instruments of Raphael Haeger, who has been a percussionist with the orchestra since 2004"--Film page.
a portrait = Thomas Leyendecker im Porträt /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.

"The subject of this film from the Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments series is the trombonist Thomas Leyendecker, who has been a member of the orchestra since 2006. Here, the musician tells why he chose such a bulky brass instrument as the trombone, and why his participation in the Philharmoniker's education projects and church activities are so important to him"--Film page.
a portrait = Michael Hasel im Porträt /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.

"The real challenge of his instrument is less in the fingering technique than in phrasing and breathing and the search for beauty of sound - according to flautist Michael Hasel who is featured in this short film in the series The Berliner Philharmoniker and their Instruments. To help him achieve his musical ideal, he has a wooden flute, made especially for him, which is also presented here"--Film page.
a portrait = Sarah Willis im Porträt /
a film by Benedikt von Bernstorff and Jakob von Bernstorff.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Benedikt von Bernstorff, director ; Jakob von Bernstorff, director and editor.

"Horn player Sarah Willis has been a member of the Berliner Philharmoniker since 2001 and is also known to audiences as a charming and humorous presenter of children's concerts. She also regularly appears in the Digital Concert Hall as an interviewer of the Philharmoniker's guest soloists and conductors. In this short film portrait, which also features the musician in excerpts from concerts, Willis explains how she decided on the French horn as her instrument when she was 14"--Film page.
a portrait = Gábor Tarkövi im Porträt /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Torben Jacobsen, director ; Uli Peschke, editor.

"Hardly any orchestral instrument is as exposed as the trumpet. In this film, Gábor Tarkövi - principal trumpet with the Berliner Philharmoniker since 2005 - explains that a trumpeter has to have good nerves. He also describes how the sound of a gold or silver-finished trumpet differs, and talks about his love of folk music and Mahler's symphonies, in which the trumpet often has a special status"--Film page.
a portrait = Alexander von Puttkamer im Porträt /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Alexander von Puttkamer, tuba and interviewee.

"Alexander von Puttkamer's instrument puts him in a unique position with the Berliner Philharmoniker: since 2008, he has been the orchestra's only permanent tuba player. In this portrait, he talks about how his love for the brass section's deepest instrument slowly developed, and he demonstrates the importance in mastering the tuba of exercising the lips"--Film page.
a portrait = Albrecht Mayer im Porträt /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.

"Oboist Albrecht Mayer is not only one of the Berliner Philharmoniker's familiar faces, but also regularly appears in the classical music charts as a solo performer. This film portrait gives you the opportunity to get to know the artist. He explains, for example, why the oboe is one of the most challenging and physically demanding instruments of all, and why he never wanted to play anything else"--Film page.
a portrait = Janne Saksala im Porträt /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Janne Saksala, double bass and interviewee.
Torben Jacobsen, director ; Uli Peschke, editor.

"Although it is difficult to overlook the symphony orchestra's largest instruments, the significance of the double bass is often underestimated. As we learn from Janne Saksala in this film portrait, they give decisive rhythmic and articulatory impulses. "Without a bass, there's no music," explains the Finn who has been 1st principal double bass with the Berliner Philharmoniker since 1994"--Film page.
a portrait = Stefan Dohr im Porträt /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Torben Jacobsen, director ; Uli Peschke, editor.

"The French horn is closely associated with the expressive world of the Romantic period due to its warm and mysterious sound. One of the outstanding players of this instrument is Stefan Dohr, principal horn with the Berliner Philharmoniker since 1993, who you can get to know in this short portrait from the series The Berliner Philharmoniker and their Instruments"--Film page.
a film by Isabel Iturriagagoitia and Paul Smaczny.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
In English and in German with English subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Berliner Philharmoniker ; Claudio Abbado, Daniel Barenboim, Bernard Haitink, Lorin Maazel, Zubin Mehta, Sir Simon Rattle, Esa-Pekka Salonen, conductors and interviewees.
Isabel Iturriagagoitia, Paul Smaczny, directors ; Steffan Herrmann, Sylvia Seuboth-Radtke, editors.

"The Berliner Philharmoniker are the world's oldest self-governing orchestra of international standing. But what does this mean in practice? The answer is revealed by this film, which follows Sir Simon Rattle's election as Claudio Abbado's successor and accompanies the orchestra from the time of Abbado's announcement that he was stepping down in February 1998 to the decision in favour of Rattle in June 1999"--Film page.
a film by Daniel Finkernagel and Magdalena Zięba-Schwind.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
In English and in German with English subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Berliner Philharmoniker ; Sir Simon Rattle, conductor and interviewee.
Magdalena Zięba-Schwind, director ; Daniel Finkernagel, writer ; Uli Peschke, editor.

"The performance of all of Beethoven's symphonies in the autumn of 2015 was a high point in the collaboration of the Berliner Philharmoniker and its chief conductor, Sir Simon Rattle. The film Living with Beethoven accompanies the ascent of this "Mount Everest" (Rattle) with many views behind the scenes. The conductor and orchestra musicians all contribute, clearly inspired by the desire to make the often performed works sound again as if they were just written"--Film page.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Wolfgang Becker, director ; Regina Ergang, Ute A. Rall, editor.

"From 1945 to 1952 Sergiu Celibidache successfully accompanied the Berliner Philharmoniker through a particularly turbulent period in post-war German history. But Berlin audiences then had to wait almost forty years before this magician of the baton returned to conduct the Berliner Philharmoniker again on 31 March 1992. The present documentary accompanies conductor and orchestra on their journey towards this memorable concert, which featured a performance of Bruckner's Seventh Symphony. A fascinating record indeed!"--Film page.
the first year = Abbado in Berlin : Das erste Jahr /
a film by Bob Eisenhardt, Susan Froemke and Peter Gelb.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
In English and in German with English subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Berliner Philharmoniker ; Claudio Abbado, conductor and interviewee.
Bob Eisenhardt, Susan Froemke and Peter Gelb, directors.

"The vote was over, and a decision had been made: Claudio Abbado was the new chief conductor of the Berliner Philharmoniker. So it was in 1989, when - for the first time - the orchestra itself decided who was to be its artistic director. The result was a surprise for the music world - and also for Abbado himself. This documentary from 1990 traces the first year of the new chief conductor in Berlin, the special atmosphere of new beginnings and the diverse expectations the musicians had of Herbert von Karajan's successor"--Film page.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Members of the Berliner Philharmoniker, instrumentalists and interviewees.

"The director Michael Wende, well-known for his highly original documentaries about classical music, has taken on the task of portraying the Berliner Philharmoniker and concert life in an altogether new light. The result are three innovative short films which include the participation of the orchestral musicians as protagonists in an unusual role"--Film page.
the pianist Menahem Pressler = "Das Leben, das ich liebe" : Der Pianist Menahem Pressler /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
In English and in German with English subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Menahem Pressler, instrumentalist and interviewee ; Daniel Harding, conductor and interviewee ; Daniel Hope, violin and interviewee.
Grete Liffers, director ; Julia Karg, editor.

"As the pianist of the famous Beaux Arts Trio for over 50 years, Menahem Pressler wrote chamber music history - until 2008 when, at over 80 years of age, he started on an amazing solo career. With his heartfelt, uniquely expressive piano tone, Pressler has since then excited audiences around the world in recitals and orchestral concerts. Our film portrait seeks to find out more about this fascinating musician and man"--Film page.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
In English and in German with English subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Berliner Philharmoniker ; Guy Braunstein, violin and interviewee ; Amnon Weinstein, Anita Lasker-Wallfisch, interviewees.
Katrin Sandmann, director ; Jakob Kastner, editor.

"In January 2015, on the 70th anniversary of the liberation of Auschwitz, musicians of the Berliner Philharmoniker played violins that had once belonged to victims of the Holocaust. In this film we meet the people who made this extraordinary concert possible - and of course the instruments themselves, each of which has its own moving story to tell"--Film page.
Maestro for the screen = Filmstar Karajan /
a film by Georg Wübbolt and Peter Gelb.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
In English and in German with English subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.

Berliner Philharmoniker ; Herbert von Karajan, conductor ; Albert Calvert, voice over ; Alfred Altenburger, Thomas Brandis, Günther Breest, Humphrey Burton, Fritz Buttenstedt, Klaus Geitel, Dietrich Gerhardt, Clemens Hellsberg, Günter Hermanns, Horant H. Hohlfeld, Herbert Kloiber, Ewald Markl, Hugo Niebeling, Norio Ohaga, Werner Resel, Lore Salzburger, Peter Schmidl, Hellmut Stern, Ernst Wild, Friedrich Witt.
Georg Wübbolt, writer and director ; Janine Dauterich, editor.

"Herbert von Karajan was not only a brilliant musician, but also a visionary in terms of his use of media to distribute his art. He was the first major conductor to recognise the enormous potential of television for classical music. This film by Georg Wübbolt traces how, step by step, Karajan made the medium his own and developed his own audiovisual aesthetic"--Film page.
Cathedrals of culture - Wim Wenders's film portrait of the Philharmonie =
Kathedralen der Kultur : Wim Wenders' Filmporträt der Philharmonie /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Berliner Philharmoniker ; Sir Simon Rattle, conductor ; Meret Becker, voice over.
Wim Wenders, writer and director ; Toni Froschhammer, editor ; Christian Rein, director of photography ; Joséphine Derobe, director of stereography.

"The Berlin Philharmonie is one of the most famous concert halls of our time. The multi-award winning filmmaker Wim Wenders presents his vision of the hall: its architecture, its history, and the people who work and perform there. With wonderful images, Wenders shows the Philharmonie from unusual perspectives, from its attics to the office of chief conductor Sir Simon Rattle"--Film page.
the Karajan Academy of the Berliner Philharmoniker = Lizenz zum Spielen : Die Karajan-Akademie der Berliner Philharmoniker /
a film by Daniel Finkernagel and Alexander Lück.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
In English and in German with English subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.

Berliner Philharmoniker ; Karajan Academy of the Berliner Philharmoniker ; Sir Simon Rattle, conductor and interviewee ; Eberhard von Koerber, Robert Gutsche, Bernhard Freiherr von Loeffelholz, interviewees.
Daniel Finkernagel and Alexander Lück, directors ; Ginny Lange, editor.

"How do you actually become a member of the Berliner Philharmoniker? One particularly promising way is through the orchestra's own academy, founded in 1972 by Herbert von Karajan. Here, outstanding musicians are methodically prepared for the work in a top orchestra. Our documentary presents this unique institution"--Film page.
beauty as I see it = Karajan : Schönheit, wie ich sie sehe /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
In German with English subtitles and in English with German subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.

Herbert von Karajan, conductor and interviewee ; Brigitte Fassbaender, Klaus Geitel, Clemens Hellsberg, Ioan Holender, Gundula Janowitz, Mariss Jansons, Fritz Kaiser, Joachim Kaiser, Arabel von Karajan, Isabel Karajan, Evgeny Kissin, René Kollo, Christa Ludwig, Ewald Markl, Yehudi Menuhin, Anne-Sophie Mutter, Seiji Ozawa, Sir Simon Rattle, Werner Resel, Helmut Schmidt, Georg Solti, Christian Thielemann, interviewees.
Robert Dornhelm, director ; Christoph Engel, editor.

"The phenomenon that was Herbert von Karajan continues to fascinate us to this day. In his documentary, Robert Dornhelm attempts to identify the man behind the image. Drawing on a wealth of little known archive material, together with statements from colleagues and family members, he creates a multi-layered portrait"--Film page.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.

Herbert von Karajan, conductor and interviewee ; Peter Alward, David Bell, Brigitte Fassbaender, Eberhard Finke, Wolfgang Gülich, Anne-Sophie Mutter, Krzysztof Polonek, Ernst Pöppel, Godehard Pruin, Klaus Stoll, Hans Weber, Andrew Wedman, interviewees.
Eric Schulz, director ; Peter Klum, editor.

"'What remains of Karajan?' asks the award-winning documentary film maker Eric Schulz. In his film Karajan - The Second Life, he explores the man and the musician - and especially his media legacy. We discover the conditions under which the technology enthusiast Karajan created his countless recordings and how these documents can help us to understand the phenomenon that is Karajan"--Film page.
Lang Lang with the Berliner Philharmoniker = The Highest Level : Lang Lang bei den Berliner Philharmonikern /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
In English and in German with English subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Berliner Philharmoniker ; Sir Simon Rattle, conductor ; Lang Lang, piano and interviewee ; Erik Hansen, narrator.
Christian Berger, director ; Stefan Kobe, editor.

"In April 2013, the Berliner Philharmoniker, Sir Simon Rattle and Lang Lang came together for their first joint recording. On the programme: Béla Bartók's Piano Concerto No. 2 and Sergei Prokofiev's Third Piano Concerto. In our documentary The Highest Level, you can be witness to this summit meeting and with extensive behind-the-scenes footage, follow of the making of a great recording"--Film page.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
In English and in German with English subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Berliner Philharmoniker ; Sir Simon Rattle, conductor.
Beijing, Seoul, Shanghai ; Hong Kong ; Taipei ; Tokyo.
Thomas Grube, director ; Martin Hoffmann, editor.

"The world of the Berliner Philharmoniker: 128 musicians, all masters of their craft who, in the service of a common purpose, combine their individual virtuosity to form the orchestra's sound. What are the human and artistic secrets that make this possible? Director Thomas Grube explores this question in his film Trip to Asia"--Film page.
a film by Thomas Grube and Enrique Sánchez Lansch.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
In English and in German with English subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Berliner Philharmoniker ; Sir Simon Rattle, conductor and interviewee ; Suz Broughton, Martin Eisentraut, Royston Maldoom, Olayinka Shitu, Marie Theinert, interviewees.
Thomas Grube, Enrique Sánchez Lansch, directors ; Dirk Grau, Martin Hoffmann.

"Rarely has a documentary about classical music met with such a response or received so many awards as the film Rhythm Is It! which has been shown in cinemas all round the world. This film is not only about great music from the past, but about things that concern all of us: How do young people develop their talents? How can creativity change all of our lives? There are also stunning dance scenes and fantastic music played by the Berliner Philharmoniker, conducted by Sir Simon Rattle"--Film page.
the silence that follows the music = Claudio Abbado : Die Stille nach der Musik /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.

Berliner Philharmoniker ; Chamber Orchestra of Europe ; Gustav Mahler Jugendorchester ; Claudio Abbado, conductor and interviewee ; Daniel Barenboim, Pierre Boulez, Zubin Mehta, Maria João Pires, Maximilian Schell, interviewees.
Paul Smaczny, writer, producer and director ; Nyika Jancso, director of photography ; Peter Pfanner, editor.

"This documentary about Claudio Abbado from the year 1996 is as much a journey through the recent history of the Berliner Philharmoniker. Director Paul Smaczny shows how the orchestra re-adjusted itself after the end of the Karajan era - such as in electing Abbado as chief conductor, whose friendly approach redefined communication and the artistic work of the orchestra"--Film page.
a film by Enrique Sánchez Lansch.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
In English and in German with English subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.

Hans Bastiaan, Sergiu Celibidache, Wilhelm Furtwängler, Erich Hartmann, Mariele Höfer-Szepes, Erich Kleiber, Hans Knapperstbusch, Ursula Küllmar, Ingrid Lorenz, Andreas Röhn, John Schuster, Hellmut Stern, interviewees.
Enrique Sánchez Lansch, writer and director ; Thomas Wellman, editor.

"In 2007, the Berliner Philharmoniker celebrated their 125th anniversary. Film director Enrique Sánchez Lansch took this occasion to tell a hitherto unknown chapter in the history of the Berliner Philharmoniker: the years of National Socialism from 1933 to 1945. The film, The Reichsorchester, made in collaboration with musicians of the orchestra and its archive, caused a stir in cinemas around the world"--Film page.
I am lost to the world /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.

Berliner Philharmoniker, Bayerisches Staatsorchester, NDR Elbphilharmonie Orchestra, Vienna Philharmonic ; Carlos Kleiber, conductor ; Ileana Cotrubas, Michael Gielen, Riccardo Muti, Wolfgang Sawallisch, interviewees ; Peter Arp, speaker
Georg Wübbolt, director ; Janine Dauterch, editor.

"Even during his lifetime, Carlos Kleiber was a myth: a conductor who was adored by audiences and colleagues. Yet he was haunted by self-doubt, and picked up the baton only reluctantly and - as the years went by - less and less frequently. In this documentary, Georg Wübbolt attempts to show what made Kleiber the phenomenon he was"--Film page.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Elisabeth Furtwängler, Frederik Malmqvist, Roberto Saccà, Ingolf Turban, interviewees.
Jan Schmidt-Garre, director ; Peter Przygodda, Wolfgang Weigl, editor.

"For us today, Wilhelm Furtwängler is a monument: immense and unapproachable. The documentary Furtwängler's love from 2004 provides us with the opportunity to get to know the man behind the conductor. At the centre is his wife Elisabeth, who gives a vivid and thoughtful account of her years at Furtwängler's side, full of charm and wit"--Film page.
The Berlin Philharmonie - A pentagon with aura =
Die Philharmonie Berlin - Ein Fünfeck mit Aura /
a film by Daniel Finkernagel and Alexander Lück.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
In English and in German with English subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Berliner Philharmoniker ; Sir Simon Rattle, conductor and interviewee ; Anna Thalbach, Helmut Gauss, speakers.
Daniel Finkernagel and Alexander Lück, writers and directors ; Uli Peschke, editor.

"In October 2013, the Berlin Philharmonie celebrated its 50th birthday - a wonderful opportunity to present a portrait of the world-famous concert hall. What is the secret of this building which is held by musicians from all over the world, and of course especially by the Berlin Philharmoniker, with such undivided affection? The documentary The Berlin Philharmonie - a pentagon with aura attempts to answer this question"--Film page.
a portrait = Wieland Welzel im Porträt /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.

"A timpanist needs nerves of steel. Every entry must be absolutely precise if he doesn't want to annoy the audience (and his fellow musicians). In this portrait of the Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments series, Wieland Welzel, timpanist with the orchestra since 1997, tells of the special requirements of his instrument - and of the opportunities to make the timpani sing"--Film page.
a portrait = Noah Bendix-Balgley im Porträt /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.

"The concertmaster has a particularly responsible role to play in the orchestra. He not only has to master extremely virtuoso solo passages - such as in the tone poems of Richard Strauss - but he also facilitates communication between conductor and orchestra. You can find out more in this short portrait of Noah Bendix-Balgley who took over this position with the Berliner Philharmoniker in 2014"--Film page.
a portrait = Dominik Wollenweber im Porträt /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Dominik Wollenweber, English horn and interviewee.

"The cor anglais, is given important solo passages in works by composers such as Richard Wagner, Hector Berlioz and Dmitri Shostakovich. Since 1993, Wollenweber has dedicated himself to this soulful instrument with the Berliner Philharmoniker. In this film portrait, he speaks about the character of his instrument, the painstaking, hand-crafted production of the mouthpiece, and the challenge of appearing in Digital Concert Hall broadcasts in front of thousands of spectators"--Film page.
a portrait = Andreas Ottensamer im Porträt /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.

"In this portrait from the series The Berliner Philharmoniker and their Instruments, Andreas Ottensamer, principal clarinet with the Berliner Philharmoniker since 2011, explains the characteristics of his instrument which was made specifically for him in Vienna, the differences between Austrian and French clarinets, and talks about the specific challenge of fading notes and making them appear as if from nowhere"--Film page.
a portrait = Máté Szűcs im Porträt /
a film by Sibylle Strobel and Torben Jacobsen.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Sibylle Strobel, Torben Jacobsen, directors ; Uli Peschke, editor.

"Máté Szűcs became 1st principal viola with the Berliner Philharmoniker in 2011. In this film portrait of the Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments series, he explains why he gave up his first instrument, the violin, in favour of the viola, and talks about his love of nature and his attachment to the music of his native Hungary"--Film page.
a portrait = Ludwig Quandt im Porträt /
a film by Sibylle Strobel and Torben Jacobsen.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Sibylle Strobel, Torben Jacobsen, directors ; Uli Peschke, editor.

"Ludwig Quandt became a member of the Berliner Philharmoniker in 1991, and in 1993, he was appointed 1st principal cellist. In this portrait, Ludwig Quandt talks about his favourite parts in orchestral works, the unique sound of the cello, which resembles the human voice - and of course his own instrument, which was built by Francesco Ruggieri in Cremona in the 17th century"--Film page.
the Easter Festival in Baden-Baden = Der Zauber des Anfangs : Die Osterfestspiele in Baden-Baden /
a film by Hannah Dorn and Jakob von Bernstorff.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
In English and in German with English subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Berliner Philharmoniker ; Sir Simon Rattle, conductor and interviewee ; Robert Carsen, Frank Elstner, Simon Halsey, Thomas Quasthoff, Kate Royal, interviewees.
Hannah Dorn, director ; Uli Peschke, editor.

"The year 2013 marked a turning point in the history of the Berliner Philharmoniker: Four decades after the founding of the Easter Festival, the orchestra transferred its very own spring residence from Salzburg to Baden-Baden. A new beginning with new artistic challenges in a new environment. Our documentary A magical beginning accompanies the Berliner Philharmoniker to the first Baden-Baden Easter Festival"--Film page.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
In English and in German with English subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.

Berliner Philharmoniker ; Karajan Academy of the Berliner Philharmoniker ; Chamber music ensembles of the Berliner Philharmoniker ; Berlin Liebhaberorchester ; Sir Simon Rattle, conductor and interviewee ; Alfred Brendel, piano and interviewee ; Cameron Carpenter, organ and interviewee ; Olli Dittrich, presenter and interviewee.
Alexander Lück, director ; Uli Peschke, editor.

"Many thousands of people attended the 'Open House' day at the Philharmonie in Berlin in May 2012 and saw the concert hall and the Berliner Philharmoniker from new, surprising perspectives. You can now relive that special day in a film"--Film page.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
In English and in German with English subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Berliner Philharmoniker ; Sir Simon Rattle, conductor ; Sarah Willis, presenter.
Alexander Lück, director ; Uli Peschke, editor.

"The Berlin Philharmonie is a landmark of Berlin's cultural scene. But there are also other outstanding institutions of art and knowledge in its immediate vicinity, all grouped around the Kulturforum where a large festival was held in June 2014. The finale of the event was a performance of Carl Orff's Carmina Burana with the Berliner Philharmoniker and Sir Simon Rattle. In our documentary, Philharmoniker horn player Sarah Willis guides us through the festival"--Film page.
a composition for 1000 voices by David Lang = Crowd Out : Eine Komposition für 1000 Stimmen von David Lang /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
David Lang, composer and interviewee ; Simon Halsey, artistic director ; Jasmina Hadziahmetovic, stage director.
Alexander Lück, director ; Uli Peschke, editor.

"Crowds are fascinating. The many different emotions and sounds that arise from crowds are incorporated by composer David Lang in his work Crowd Out. More than 1,000 choristers performed Crowd Out at the festival at the Kulturforum in the summer of 2014 - an exciting experiment that you can relive in this documentary"--Film page.
Herbert von Karajan conducts Wagner's "Das Rheingold" = Herbert von Karajan dirigiert Wagners "Rheingold" /
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.

Thomas Stewart as Wotan ; Brigitte Fassbaender as Fricka ; Peter Schreier as Loge ; Zoltan Kelemen as Alberich ; Jeannine Altmeyer as Freia ; Gergard Stolze as Mime ; Gerd Nienstedt as Fasolt ; Louis Hendrikx as Fafner ; Hermin Esser as Froh ; Vladimir de Kanel as Donner ; Birgit Finnilä as Erda ; Eva Randová as Woglinde ; Edda Moser as Wellgunde ; Liselotte Rebmann as Flosshilde ; Herbert von Karajan, conductor ; Berliner Philharmoniker.
Music and stage director, Herbert von Karajan ; Director of photography, Ernst Wild ; Set and costume design, Georges Wakhevitch ; Projections, Johannes Dreher ; Architect, Utz Elsässer.

Herbert von Karajan, a renowned Wagner conductor known for his lyrical, fiery, and contrapuntally clear interpretation of Wagner's music, diverged from the traditional "German" school of conducting, drawing inspiration from conductors like Toscanini and Clemens Krauss. Karajan conducted four complete Ring cycles during his career in Aachen (1937), Bayreuth (1951), Vienna (1959), and at his Salzburg Easter Festival (1967-1970) with the Berliner Philharmoniker, his preferred ensemble. In 1973, he began filming a complete Salzburg Ring, featuring Thomas Stewart as Wotan, Brigitte Fassbaender as Fricka, and Peter Schreier as Loge. Despite completing the soundtrack in 1973, the full cinema version proved unaffordable, leaving only the Salzburg Das Rheingold as the sole remaining film record of Karajan's Wagner productions among numerous recorded and illustrated productions.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.

Jon Vickers as Otello ; Mirella Freni as Desdemona ; Peter Glossop as Jago ; Aldo Bottion as Cassio ; Stefania Malagù as Emilia ; Michel Sénéchal as Rodrigo ; Mario Macchi as Montano ; José van Dam as Lodovico ; Herbert von Karajan, conductor ; Berliner Philharmoniker ; Chorus of Deutsche Oper Berlin.
Herbert von Karajan, director ; Roger Benamou, director ; Walter Hagen-Groll, chorus master ; Günter Schneider-Siemessen, costume and set designer ; Georges Wakhevitch, costume designer.

Herbert von Karajan's 1970-72 Salzburg Festival production of Verdi's Otello, preserved in the historically important 1973 film co-directed by Roger Benamou, featured one of the greatest casts and productions of the twentieth century. Jon Vickers portrayed Otello with modern psychological insights and trumpet-like tones, impressing Karajan as the ultimate interpreter of the role. His scenes with the lyric soprano Mirella Freni as Desdemona in the final acts were intensely moving. Peter Glossop, known for his piercing eyes and dramatic intelligence, portrayed Iago in the English tradition, embodying the embittered soldier of Shakespeare's tragedy. Set and costume designs by Günther Schneider-Siemssen and Georges Wakhevitch, along with the Berlin Philharmoniker's exceptional performance, contributed to the production's brilliance. Despite technical challenges in the original cinema release, digital remastering has now resolved these issues, ensuring its enduring legacy.
a portrait = Martin Heinze im Portr at /
The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments =
Die Berliner Philharmoniker und ihre instrumente
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Featuring Martin Heinze, double bass and interviewee.
Video director, Sybille Stroble ; Director of photography, Thomas Kutschker ; Colourist, Jan Hartman ; Subtitles, texthouse.

"Martin Heinze came to his instrument by chance: he actually liked the jazz organ, but the music school offered him the chance to learn the double bass instead. A stroke of luck, as our portrait from the series The Berliner Philharmoniker and their instruments shows. Martin Heinze also explains why he is the only member of the orchestra's double bass section to have opted for gut strings, and what effect they have on the sound"--film page
In German with English subtitles or English with German subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Kirill Petrenko, conductor ; Raphael Haeger, conductor ; Daishin Kashimoto, violin ; Ludwig Quandt, cello ; Members of the Berliner Philharmoniker ; Members of the Karajan Academy.
Director, Magdalena Zieba-Schwind ; Director, Adam Janisch.

"In 2024, a dream came true for around 100 amateur musicians in Japan, when they took to the stage of Tokyo's famous Suntory Hall as the Be Phil Orchestra with Kirill Petrenko and members of the Berliner Philharmoniker. After intensive rehearsals together, they presented a concert featuring works by Prokofiev and Brahms. The film documents the education project and the great love of music-making shown by everyone involved"--Film page.
The Berliner Philharmoniker and Unsuk Chin = Kl ange aus licht und farben : Die Berliner Philharmoniker und Unsuk Chin /
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Originally published as part of The Unsuk Chin Edition.
Director, Timo Hagemeister ; video editing, Erik Brauer ; video editing, Jakob von Bernstorff.

"Unsuk Chin draws inspiration from natural phenomena such as the interplay of light and colour. The Berliner Philharmoniker have dedicated The Unsuk Chin Edition to their extensive collaboration with the composer. In the bonus film accompanying the edition, the South Korean-born composer talks about her new beginning in Europe, her lessons with G yrgy Ligeti and the search for her own musical voice"--Film page.
The visionary Claudio Abbado = "Please call me Claudio" : Der vision ar Claudio Abbado.
In German or Italian with English or German subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Members and former members of the Berliner Philharmoniker.

Director, Beatrix Conrad ; editor, Nils Plambeck ; producer, Isabel Iturriagagoita Bueno ; executive producer, Jan Bremme ; executive producer, Pierre-Fran cois Decoufl e ; executive producer, Bernd Hellthaler

"An artist with great charisma: Claudio Abbado not only shaped the Berliner Philharmoniker as chief conductor from 1990 to 2002, but also laid invaluable foundations for orchestral culture through his work with youth orchestras. The documentary provides moving insights into his work and above all shows Claudio Abbado the man, who in all modesty preferred to be called simply "Claudio""--Film page.
15 years of the Digital Concert Hall :
the anniversary broadcast = 15 Jahre Digital Concert Hall - die Jubil aumssendung /
In English, German, Japanese, or Korean with English and/or German subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Sarah Willis, speaker ; Sol ene Kermarrec, speaker ; Knut Weber, speaker ; Stefan Dohr, speaker ; Matthew McDonald, speaker ; Berliner Philharmoniker.
Director, Katharina Bernstone ; director, Magdalena Zieba-Schwind ; video editor, Uli Peschke ; video editor, Adam Janisch ; video editor, Jakob von Bernstorff ; video editor, Friedrich Kille.

"The launch of the Berliner Philharmoniker's Digital Concert Hall in 2008 was the result of an "adventurous and actually crazy idea", according to principal cellist and founder Olaf Maninger. In a major anniversary broadcast, presenter and horn player Sarah Willis, other members of the Berliner Philharmoniker and special guests will take an entertaining look back, ahead, and behind the scenes - from the first live broadcast and numerous musical highlights to the latest technical developments. The digital world was still in its infancy when the Berliner Philharmoniker first ventured into new technological territory: to make itself independent of struggling record companies and TV broadcasts, the orchestra itself became a producer. The concerts were to be shown and documented in the best sound and image quality possible on a dedicated internet platform: the Digital Concert Hall. Olaf Maninger first had to do some convincing - even a world-class orchestra like the Berliner Philharmoniker can be shy of the camera. The orchestra agreed, but on the condition that neither recording equipment nor lighting would intrude on the concerts. The innovative solution: instead of cameras on the stage, permanently installed, silent remote-controlled cameras provided the images from the auditorium right from the start. In 2008, the vision became reality - and now the Digital Concert Hall is celebrating its 15th anniversary this season. Reason enough to take a look back together: at 15 years of online orchestral history and a selection of special events relating to the Digital Concert Hall"--Film page.
Parts 1 to 3 = Die Philly Show : Teile 1 bis 3 /
Michael Wende, director, Witja Frank, producer.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Anna Thalbach, as Philly ; Berliner Philharmoniker ; Kirill Petrenko, conductor ; Lahav Shani, conductor ; Susanna M alkki, conductor ; Sir Simon Rattle, conductor
Director, Michael Wende ; editor, Hanni Welter ; producer, Witja Frank.

"Philly is bright yellow and has five corners - and has its own quiz show. It involves two teams who compete against each other and have to answer questions about music and the Berliner Philharmoniker - which isn't so easy! Who knows where the name cor anglais comes from? Philly also dives into the depths of instruments and takes a close look at everything from the inside. Great musical fun for the whole family!"--film page.
a year in the Karajan Academy = Klangw utig : ein Jahr an der Karajan-Akademie.
In German, English, or Spanish with English subtitles.
German version has no subtitles and is instead overdubbed.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Director, Isabel Hahn ; director, Silvia P almigiano ; editor, Agata Welz ; producer, Elke Sasse ; exective producer, Christopher Janssen.

"Places at the Karajan Academy of the Berliner Philharmoniker are highly coveted among young musicians. But those like conductor Nodoka Okisawa, violist Sara Fer rndez and trumpeter Lennard Czakaj who have earned a place there are still a long way from reaching their goal. The film, which was made for the 50th anniversary of the school for young talents, accompanies the three scholarship holders, who are confronted with very different desires and challenges"--Film page.
Anton Bruckner and the Berliner Philharmoniker :
conductors in conversation = Anton Bruckner und die Berliner Philharmoniker : Gespräche mit Dirigenten.
In German with English subtitles or English with German subtitles.
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Bernard Haitink ; Zubin Mehta ; Sir Simon Rattle ; Herbert Blomstedt ; Christian Thielemann ; Mariss Jansons ; Paavo Järvi ; Benedikt von Bernstorff, director.

"Anton Bruckner's darkly glowing, overwhelmingly beautiful and astonishingly innovative tonal language is unmistakable. His music has been part of the Berliner Philharmoniker's artistic identity for over a hundred years. In our film, specialists such as Herbert Blomstedt, Bernard Haitink and Christian Thielemann talk about Bruckner's symphonies, which they have performed together with the orchestra and which Berliner Philharmoniker Recordings has released in a CD edition"--Film page.
A vision for Europe = Lisa Batiashvili : Vision für Europa /
Title from film page and Berlin Phil Media GmbH.
Lisa Batiashvili ; Kirill Petrenko ; Daniel Harding.

"Music's message shouldn't just stay in the concert hall - I want it to be heard and seen beyond that." Violinist Lisa Batiashvili, the Berliner Philharmoniker's 2023/24 Artist in Residence, is not only a gifted soloist and chamber musician, but also an artist with a keen political awareness. Our film portrait accompanies her during her residency - including to her Georgian homeland, where she performed in the Europakonzert "filled with feelings of happiness"
